LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF DAVID WHITE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS. 1, 6, AND 7 IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT DID NOT WAIVE OBJECTIONS. HOWEVER, DEFENDANT FAILED IN ITS OBLIGATION TO PROVIDE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO A REASONABLE CONSTRUCTION OF THE INTERROGATORIES. (SEE DEYO V. KILBOURNE (1978) 84 CAL.APP.3D 71, 783.) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NOS. 2 AND 13 IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NOS. 15, 18, 19, 20, 29, 30, 34, 35, AND 38 IS GRANTED. WITHIN TEN DAYS, DEFENDANTS ARE TO PROVIDE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO THE INTERROGATORIES AND DOCUMENT REQUESTS LISTED. BOTH PARTIES' REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. (SEE ORDER FOR MORE DETAILS). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-24-2015 Vacated

Hearing Of Defendants' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL SCHREIBER OF BRACAMONTES & VLASAK, TEL. NO. 835-6777 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ABRAM P. PETERSEN OF LUBIN/OLSON, TEL. NO. 981-0550 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT 540 LEAVENWORTH LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Plaintiff David White's Motion to compel further responses to plaintiff's special interrogatories, set one and request for Production of Documents, Set one and request for sanctions - Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Special Interrogatory Nos. 1, 6, and 7 is granted. Defendant did not waive objections. However, Defendant failed in its obligation to provide full and complete responses to a reasonable construction of the interrogatories. (See Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 71, 783.) Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Production of Documents Nos. 2 and 13 is denied. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Production of Documents Nos. 15, 18, 19, 20, 29, 30, 34, 35, and 38 is granted. Within ten days, Defendants are to provide full and complete responses to the interrogatories and document requests listed. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. (See Order for more details). Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/EG

Hearing Of Defendants' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-08-2014 Continued To Sep-22-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Schreiber, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak (415-835-6777) for Plaintiff and moving party David White.
Abram Petersen, Esq. of Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski LLP (415-981-0550) for Defendants and opposing party Kobe Royo and Afsaneh Royo.

Defendant's Counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem.
ARGUED. Hearing Of Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions is continued to September 22, 2014 at 9:30 am in Department 302, Law and Motion Department to be heard by Judge Ernest Goldsmith. Judge Pro tem to prepare report and recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
